outcomes: Derive the integration of polynomial and simple trigonometric functions as the reverse of differentiation
Apply definite integrals to evaluate the exact area bounded under curves and between intersecting boundary lines
Formulate and solve first-order ordinary differential equations utilizing the separation of variables method
Perform algebraic operations on complex numbers ($z = a + ib$) and represent them spatially on Argand diagrams
Convert complex numbers between Cartesian form and polar configuration ($r(\cos\theta + i\sin\theta)$)
Analyze the photoelectric equation ($E = hf = W_0 + K_{max}$) and solve problems involving work function threshold frequencies
Explain the mechanics of X-ray generation within a Coolidge tube and differentiate continuous vs. characteristic spectra profiles
Formulate balanced nuclear equations for Alpha, Beta, and Gamma decays, and calculate radioisotope half-life decay constants

sub_strands.sub_strand: Electrochemical Cells and Standard Electrode Potentials
outcomes: Construct voltaic/galvanic cells, compute standard cell EMF ($E^{\theta}_{cell} = E^{\theta}_{cathode} - E^{\theta}_{anode}$), and predict reaction spontaneity
Analyze industrial electrolytic processes including the Down's cell extraction of sodium and the electrorefining of copper metal
Differentiate addition and condensation polymerization mechanics, detailing structures for nylon-6,6, Terylene, and polythene
Evaluate environmental impacts of synthetic polymers and research structural biopolymer alternatives

sub_strands.sub_strand: Evidence of Evolution and Specialization Drivers
outcomes: Critique evolutionary assertions based on comparative anatomy (homologous vs. analogous organs), embryology, and fossil timelines
Apply the Hardy-Weinberg principle equation ($p^2 + 2pq + q^2 = 1$) to compute allele frequency vectors inside static populations
